vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1827: Conversation view blank for pi-harness sessions — add pi branch to the shared transcript resolver

plan.title: Conversation view blank for pi-harness sessions — resolver handles claude-code and codex only
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: The flywheel orchestrator (kimi-k2.6 on the pi harness) and all pi-harness work/strike agents render 'No conversation data available' in the Command Deck. The shared transcript resolver src/dashboard/server/routes/jsonl-resolver.ts dispatches on the harness recorded in state.json with branches only for claude-code and codex (PAN-1805) — no pi branch — so pi agents fall through to the claude-code lookup, find no ~/.claude/projects JSONL, and resolve to null. Same bug class as PAN-1805, third harness. A second codex-aware resolver — the /api/conversations/:name/messages fallback in conversations.ts — has the same gap, and additionally its agent-name prefix guard omits 'strike-', so strike agents never even reach harness resolution.
narratives.Proposal: Mirror the codex pattern in the two resolvers that already carry a codex branch. (1) Add resolvePiSessionPath(agentId, opts) to jsonl-resolver.ts (async; freshest .jsonl under <agentDir>/sessions/ by mtime, mirroring PiRuntimeSync.getSessionPath) and dispatch a 'pi' branch in resolveJsonlPath BEFORE the claude fallback. (2) Add a 'pi' branch to the /messages fallback and extend its prefix guard to include strike-/inspect-. (3) Add regression tests in jsonl-resolver.test.ts mirroring the codex cases. The pi parser (parsePiConversationMessages + isPiSessionFile) already exists and is already reached by getCachedMessages — no new parser is written; the resolved pi path simply needs to reach that dispatch.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Do NOT modify /api/agents/:id/conversation (buildConversationResponse -> getAgentJsonlPath in agent-enrichment.ts); it is claude-only and lacks even a codex branch — a separate pre-existing gap (file a follow-up).
- Do NOT write a new pi conversation parser — parsePiConversationMessages / isPiSessionFile already exist and are reused.
- Do NOT change pi spawn, runtime, or session-file writing.
- Do NOT modify the registered-conversation path resolveSessionFile (conversations.ts:754) — it is already pi-aware (handles the flywheel orchestrator).
- Do NOT call PiRuntimeSync.getSessionPath from the resolver (it is sync readdirSync; the dashboard event loop forbids sync fs).

autoDecisions.summary: Scope limited to the two codex-mirroring resolvers (jsonl-resolver.ts resolveJsonlPath and the conversations.ts /messages fallback); the agent-enrichment getAgentJsonlPath path is out of scope.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue names 'the shared resolver' and 'like the codex rollout path does'. Both anchor on places where a codex branch already exists. agent-enrichment getAgentJsonlPath never had codex support, so replicating into it is a distinct gap, not this issue.

autoDecisions.summary: resolvePiSessionPath resolves the freshest .jsonl under <agentDir>/sessions/ by mtime, with no thread-id fast path.
autoDecisions.rationale: Mirrors PiRuntimeSync.getSessionPath (pi.ts:125). The per-agent sessions dir holds only this agent's sessions, so freshest-by-mtime is the live transcript — the same reasoning the codex path uses for per-agent CODEX_HOME. Pi filenames embed the session id but no separate thread-id mapping is needed.

autoDecisions.summary: Extend the /messages fallback prefix guard to include strike- and inspect-.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue requires covering strike agents. Strike agents are named strike-<id> (canonical AGENT_PREFIXES, agents.ts:582 = agent-/planning-/conv-/strike-/inspect-). The route hardcoded /^(specialist-|agent-|planning-)/, which excludes strike-, so the codex branch never ran for them either.

Each change is a mechanical mirror of the proven codex path. A wrong implementation surfaces immediately in the jsonl-resolver regression tests (pi-resolver-tests bead) at typecheck/test time, not as silent foundation rot.

items.title: Add resolvePiSessionPath + pi branch to resolveJsonlPath (jsonl-resolver.ts)

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/jsonl-resolver.ts add an exported async function resolvePiSessionPath(agentId, opts: ResolveJsonlPathOptions = {}) that mirrors resolveCodexRolloutPath (line 178): compute agentDir = <agentsDirOverride ?? ~/.panopticon/agents>/<agentId>, return null if <agentDir>/sessions does not exist, otherwise walk that tree with fs/promises (e.g. readdir(..., {recursive:true}) + stat), collect every *.jsonl, and return the absolute path with the newest mtime, or null when none exist. Then in resolveJsonlPath (line 204) add, immediately after the existing codex dispatch (line 211) and BEFORE the resolveClaudeSessionId call (line 215), a branch: if (await resolveAgentHarness(agentId, opts) === 'pi') return resolvePiSessionPath(agentId, opts). Combine the two harness reads into one resolveAgentHarness call to avoid a double read. Update the file-level docstring (lines 1-24) to mention the pi branch alongside codex. Use only fs/promises (no *Sync, no execSync). Do NOT import PiRuntimeSync.

items.title: resolvePiSessionPath('agent-x', {agentsDirOverride}) returns the freshest .jsonl found under <agentsDir>/agent-x/sessions/ (newest mtime), at any nesting depth
resolvePiSessionPath returns null when the agent has no sessions/ directory or it contains no .jsonl file
resolveJsonlPath returns the pi session path when the agent state.json harness is 'pi', dispatching the pi branch before the claude-code lookup

For a pi agent that also has a session.id file (a pi session id, not a claude UUID), resolveJsonlPath returns the pi sessions path and does NOT return a ~/.claude/projects/... path — the pi branch wins over the claude fallback
resolveJsonlPath still returns the codex rollout for codex agents and the claude JSONL for claude-code agents (existing dispatch preserved)
Regression tests for pi resolution in jsonl-resolver.test.ts (mirror codex cases)

narrative.Action: Add a new describe('resolveJsonlPath — pi agents (PAN-1827)') block to src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/jsonl-resolver.test.ts mirroring the existing codex describe (line 206). Build a pi agent fixture under agentsDir: state.json with harness:'pi', and a sessions/ subtree containing one or more .jsonl files with staggered mtimes via utimes (reuse the codex block's staggering pattern so 'freshest' is deterministic). Cover: resolves the freshest pi session JSONL; returns null when sessions/ is empty / absent; pi harness wins over a stale claude session.id + matching ~/.claude/projects JSONL; resolvePiSessionPath returns null for an agent without a sessions/ dir. Use real fs/promises temp dirs like the existing tests (no fake timers needed — these are not delay/retry tests).

items.title: A test asserts resolveJsonlPath returns the newest-mtime pi session .jsonl for a pi-harness agent fixture
A test asserts resolveJsonlPath returns null for a pi agent whose sessions/ directory has no .jsonl yet

A test asserts the pi harness wins over a stale claude session.id (mirrors the codex 'harness wins over stale claude session.id' case): with both a pi sessions JSONL and a ~/.claude/projects JSONL present, resolveJsonlPath returns the pi path
A test asserts resolvePiSessionPath returns null for an agent that has no sessions/ directory
Add pi branch + strike-/inspect- prefixes to the /messages agent fallback (conversations.ts)

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts, in the GET /api/conversations/:name/messages route's no-conversation fallback (lines 3009-3066): (1) extend the prefix guard regex on line 3013 from /^(specialist-|agent-|planning-)/ to /^(specialist-|agent-|planning-|strike-|inspect-)/ so strike and inspect agents reach harness resolution. (2) Add a pi branch mirroring the codex branch (lines 3017-3024): immediately after the codex block, add `if (!sessionFile && await resolveAgentHarness(name) === 'pi') { const piPath = await resolvePiSessionPath(name); if (piPath) { sessionFile = piPath; setSpecialistSessionCache(name, piPath); } }`. Add resolvePiSessionPath to the existing import from './jsonl-resolver.js' (line 27). The resolved pi path is parsed by the existing getCachedMessages dispatch (line 3078 -> isPiSessionFile -> parsePiConversationMessages), so no parser change is needed. Leave the codex branch and the claude-projects scan intact and in order.

items.title: When name is a pipeline agent not in the conversations DB and resolveAgentHarness(name) === 'pi', the /messages route resolves sessionFile via resolvePiSessionPath(name) and caches it via setSpecialistSessionCache
The fallback prefix guard accepts strike- and inspect- session names (e.g. strike-pan-1723), so strike agents reach harness resolution
getCachedMessages routes a resolved pi session file to parsePiConversationMessages (via isPiSessionFile), so the /messages response returns a populated messages array for a pi work/strike agent
The fallback preserves the existing codex branch and the claude-projects scan, in the same order (codex checked first, then pi, then claude scan) — no regression for codex or claude-code agents
